Tests bitwise XOR assignment and equality operations on an object with custom valueOf and toString methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which return `1` and `0`, respectively. The object is then assigned to a variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. The code performs a bitwise XOR operation on the `object` using the `^=` operator with the value `1`, and then checks two conditions using an `assert` statement. The first condition verifies that the result of the XOR operation on `object` is equal to the result of the XOR operation on `copyObject`. The second condition checks that the updated value of `object` is equal to the result of the XOR operation on `copyObject`. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating the behavior of bitwise operations and object references in JavaScript.
